justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — '99.95% internal SLA is mathematically impossible when the critical-path dependency (Onfido) only guarantees 99.9%' with exact downtime figures (4.38h vs 8.76h). (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended — async KYC with pending_kyc state, queue-and-retry fallback, and implicit SLA revision via escalation. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'the single biggest operational risk'. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling identified with specific recommendation to split liveness/readiness probes. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalations routed to Coach K for SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is front and center — identified in the very first component interaction and quantified precisely (4.38h vs 8.76h gap)
Circuit breaker absence identified with clear explanation of the cascading failure mechanism (thread/connection pool exhaustion)
Health check coupling correctly identified as a cascading outage amplifier, with actionable liveness/readiness split recommendation
Goes beyond rubric requirements — also flags idempotency key gap, rate limiting risk, PII logging concern, and missing on-call runbook
Escalations are well-structured and correctly routed, making the business decision explicit rather than making it for the team
Async KYC fallback recommendation is concrete — specifies pending_kyc user state, queue infrastructure, and webhook-based completion
